summary: Linked the mngallery menu object by converting melee/mn/mngallery.c from NonMatching to Matching, replacing placeholder local structs and extern constants with typed static data, userdata structs, static helper functions, literals, and archive section names. The PR also corrected a boundary/type ownership issue where 0x804A0B90 belonged to mnsnap rather than mngallery, updating splits, symbols, mnsnap.h, gm_1B03.c, and mn/types.h accordingly.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so Object("melee/mn/mngallery.c") is marked Matching instead of NonMatching.

Reworked src/melee/mn/mngallery.c to remove placeholder includes and many local anonymous struct definitions, replacing them with file-local typed structs such as mnGallery_804D6C88_userdata and mnGallery_child_userdata.
Added STATIC_ASSERT checks for the newly inferred userdata layouts: mnGallery_804D6C88_userdata size 0x24 and mnGallery_child_userdata size 8.
Changed mnGallery_804D6C88 from a void pointer to a static HSD_GObj pointer and added GET_804D6C88 / GET_mnGallery_child_userdata macros around HSD_GObjGetUserData casts.

Introduced static StaticModelDesc objects at mnGallery_804A0BA0 and mnGallery_804A0BB0, then used named fields like joint, animjoint, matanim_joint, and shapeanim_joint instead of treating the data as raw void pointer arrays.
Replaced several generated extern constants with direct literals or initialized locals, including camera vectors, 0.0f, 1.0f, 2.0f, 96.0f, 72.0f, and MTHP filenames "MvOmake15.mth" and "MvHowTo.mth".
Made helper routines static where no longer exported from the mngallery header, including mnGallery_80258BC4, mnGallery_80258D50, mnGallery_80258DBC, and mnGallery_80259604.
Added small matching helpers such as float_order_helper, fn_80258ED0_helper, and fn_802590C4_inline to preserve required codegen and cleanup behavior.
Updated mngallery.h to include melee/sc/forward.h and removed prototypes for now-static implementation helpers plus the incorrect mnGallery_804A0B90 extern.
Moved the 0x804A0B90 allocation/type from gallery to snap: gm_1B03.c now includes mnsnap.h and allocates mnSnap_804A0B90 entries as struct mnSnap_804A0B90_t.
Renamed struct mnGallery_804A0B90_t to struct mnSnap_804A0B90_t in mn/types.h while preserving its 0x96000 size assertion.
Updated config/GALE01/splits.txt so mnsnap.bss extends to 0x804A0BA0 and mngallery.bss begins at 0x804A0BA0; adjusted mngallery .data and .sdata ends.
Updated symbols.txt to mark many mngallery rodata/data/sdata/sdata2 constants as local compiler-style symbols and to identify mnSnap_804A0B90 at 0x804A0B90.
smart_moves: The PR solved matching by inferring concrete userdata structures and replacing repeated ad-hoc local struct overlays, making field intent clearer while retaining layout through STATIC_ASSERT.
Correcting the 0x804A0B90 symbol ownership prevented mnsnap data from being incorrectly exposed as mngallery data, aligning code, headers, symbols, and splits.
Using StaticModelDesc for gallery model archive sections made the bss globals meaningful and enabled field-based section loading instead of raw array indexing.
Several constants were localized back into the C code as literals or local compiler symbols, reducing unnecessary global symbol pollution in symbols.txt.
The direct archive section names in lbArchive_LoadSections document exactly which model and animation resources mngallery loads.
The author used targeted inline/static helper functions and dont_inline pragmas where needed instead of leaving the file full of broad optimization pragmas and placeholder structs.
decomp_lessons: When a file links, revisit neighboring split boundaries; here a bss object at 0x804A0B90 was discovered to belong to mnsnap, not mngallery.
For menu GObj userdata, replace repeated anonymous overlays with one asserted struct once enough fields are understood; this reduces accidental field-offset drift.
Do not keep address-named globals for constants that the compiler can represent as local literals or local @ symbols; doing so can improve symbol hygiene after matching.
Archive model pointer groups often want an existing descriptor type such as StaticModelDesc rather than a void* array.
Header exports should be pruned after implementation helpers become static; only externally used functions and globals should remain in the public header.
Matching sometimes requires accepting narrow codegen hacks temporarily, such as direct HSD_GObjGetUserData calls or volatile pointer casts, but these should be marked with explicit @todo comments.
If a GET_* macro changes stack/codegen, direct access to user_data or HSD_GObjGetUserData may be necessary as a local matching tactic until the helper can be improved.
naming_conventions: Use mnGallery_804D6C88_userdata for file-specific userdata tied to an address-named global when the real domain name is still unknown.
Use GET_804D6C88(gobj) and GET_mnGallery_child_userdata(gobj) macros to mirror project patterns like GET_FIGHTER while casting HSD_GObjGetUserData.
Rename misattributed address types to the owning subsystem, e.g. mnGallery_804A0B90_t became mnSnap_804A0B90_t.
Use StaticModelDesc fields named joint, animjoint, matanim_joint, and shapeanim_joint for menu model resource bundles.
Make decompiled implementation-only functions static and remove them from headers once no longer externally referenced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used STATIC_ASSERT to lock inferred struct sizes for matching-sensitive userdata layouts.
Used #pragma dont_inline around selected helper functions where inlining behavior affected matching.
Created fn_802590C4_inline as an inline cleanup helper to match the original destruction loop over child GObjs.
Used direct HSD_GObjGetUserData calls in fn_802590C4 because the review notes say GET_804D6C88 broke these cases.
Used direct mnGallery_804D6C88->user_data access in fn_80258ED0_helper with an @todo noting that GET_804D6C88 blew the stack.
Used a volatile pointer cast for data->gobj8 cleanup with an @todo marking it as hacky, indicating a codegen workaround.
Introduced float_order_helper to set sobj->x10 and sobj->x14 in an order that matched codegen.
Removed broader optimization pragmas from sections that could now match through typed code and helper extraction.
Replaced extern float/double symbols with literal constants where the matching compiler output allowed local constants.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.comment: Asked what was happening around the direct HSD_GObjGetUserData use and the @todo noting GET_804D6C88 breaks these.

review_feedback.author: jellejurre
review_feedback.comment: Explained that the issue was needing to use HSD_GObjGetUserData directly instead of the helper method defined for this file.

review_feedback.author: BR-
review_feedback.comment: Clarified that HSD_GObjGetUserData is the inline behind patterns like GET_FIGHTER and simply returns userdata through void*; GET_804D6C88 was a local macro mimicking GET_FIGHTER by calling the inline and casting to mnGallery_804D6C88_userdata*.

review_feedback.author: jellejurre
review_feedback.comment: Stated the work was about 90% done and that remaining messiness involved a mnGallery_t StaticModelDesc-like struct and figuring out the mnGallery_804D6C88 struct.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove the @todo comments around GET_804D6C88 blowing the stack or breaking fn_802590C4.
Search for later renames of mnGallery_804D6C88_userdata fields unk0, unk1, unk2, unk15, frame, gobj4, and gobj8.
Check whether mnGallery_t or StaticModelDesc usage in mngallery was generalized in subsequent menu decomp PRs.
Look for PRs that further clean up mnSnap_804A0B90 or mnsnap/mngallery bss split boundaries.
Search for other files using local GET_<address> macros modeled after GET_FIGHTER and whether reviewers preferred direct HSD_GObjGetUserData.
